title: keckiella antirrhinoides
intro: Keckiella antirrhinoides, also known as the yellow monkey flower, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Keckiella antirrhinoides include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
